release: Phase 7 — Final Live QA & Master Release
scope: Phase 1: foundation hardening — deterministic install/build, Vercel deployment contract, canonical metadata, cache discipline and local-first runtime policy.
Phase 2: premium visual system, RTL layouts, themes and mobile drawer refinement.
Phase 3: hand tracking reliability, pinch stability, drawing smoothness, camera isolation and fist continuity.
Phase 4: export, save, update, offline recovery, onboarding and privacy polish.
Phase 5: final release identity, documentation, QA manifest, source-package verification and Termux/Vercel delivery procedure.
Phase 6: privacy-preserving device-readiness diagnostics for secure context, camera API support, local hand model availability, storage, PWA, graphics and network state.
Phase 7: final live QA, passive camera/hand-state reporting, master release documents and deterministic delivery verification.
privacy: Drawing data stays local unless the creator explicitly requests an export, share or AI generation.
releaseGate: npm run vercel:build confirms the verified local model, all Phase 1–7 source contracts, self-hosted MediaPipe output and final release metadata before deployment.
notes: v6.5.0 Phase 6 adds a real browser capability report that never requests camera permission and never sends device diagnostics off-device.
The deployed browser remains local-first: hand model and MediaPipe runtime stay same-origin assets.
v7.0.0 Phase 7 adds Final Live QA: passive, local reporting of canvas, save, active camera, hand engine and recent detection state without permission prompts or telemetry.
